zh.lifeSummary: 當Per Hermansson Fleming於大約1480年出生在芬蘭阿斯凱時，他的父親Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs41歲，母親Elin Filipsdotter31歲。他和Elin Hansdotter Lydecke至少有1個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1550年在瑞典可帕波格諾貝可享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Per Hermansson Fleming于大约1480年出生在芬兰阿斯凱时，他的父亲Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs41岁，母亲Elin Filipsdotter31岁。他和Elin Hansdotter Lydecke至少有1个儿子和5个女儿。他去世于1550年在瑞典可帕波格諾貝可享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hermansson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Helmer, Anders, Bjorn, Per.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Hermansson: patronymic from the personal name Herman .
Americanized form of Danish and Norwegian Hermansen , a cognate of 1 above, and of its rare variant Hermannsen, which is also North German.

variants.name: Herman
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, Flemish, Hungarian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. Compare Harman 5.
Americanized form of German   Hermann  , a cognate of 1 above.
English: perhaps a shortened form of Herniman, a topographic name for a ‘dweller (in a) nook or corner of land’, from Middle English herne, hirne, hurne + man. See also Hearn .

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: Per
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: The Russo-Swedish War
historicEvents.description: While in an alliance with Grand Prince Ivan III of Moscow, King Hans of Denmark and Norway invaded Sweden and was declared King of Sweden.

historicEvents.title: The Stockholm Bloodbath
historicEvents.description: A mass execution of Swedish nobles by Danish king Christian II is known as the Stockholm Bloodbath. Christian II invaded Sweden in 1519 and took hold of Stockholm in September 1520. Over 80 nobles were executed for heresy on November 8 and 9. The outrage from the executions led to the Swedish removal of support of the Kalmar Union.

historicEvents.title: Swedish War of Liberation
historicEvents.description: The Swedish War of Liberation was a civil war led by nobleman Gustav Vasa. As Christian II heard of the rebellion, he confronted Vasa's men, who won the battle at Brunbacks Ferry. Christian II was defeated by the Swedes in Stockholm and a truce was agreed upon. Vasa was given as a hostage as a show of good faith until the treaty was signed. The Danish King did not keep his word and Vasa was held as a prisoner for two years until his escape. A bounty was placed on Vasa's head. Vasa worked to inspire many to join his cause and lived under disguise until the Danes surrendered in 1525.

historicEvents.title: First Full Swedish Translation of the Bible
historicEvents.description: The first full Swedish translation of the Bible, known as the Gustav Bible, was completed by Olaus Petri and Laurentius Andreae and published in 1540.

historicEvents.title: Sweden Becomes Lutheran Nation
historicEvents.description: Sweden officially declared itself a Lutheran nation in 1544.

en.lifeSummary: When Per Hermansson Fleming was born about 1480, in Askainen, Finland, his father, Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs, was 41 and his mother, Elin Filipsdotter, was 31. He had at least 1 son and 5 daughters with Elin Hansdotter Lydecke. He died in 1550, in Norrbärke, Kopparberg, Sweden, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Per Hermansson Fleming est né environ 1480, à Askainen, Finlande, son père, Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Elin Filipsdotter, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles avec Elin Hansdotter Lydecke. Il est décédé en 1550, à Norrbärke, Kopparberg, Suède, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Per Hermansson Fleming um 1480 in Askainen, Finnland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs 41 und seine Mutter Elin Filipsdotter 31. Er hatte mit Elin Hansdotter Lydecke mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Er starb 1550 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Norrbärke, Kopparberg, Schweden.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Per Hermansson Fleming è nato il circa 1480 nel Askainen, Finlandia, il suo padre, Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Elin Filipsdotter, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie con Elin Hansdotter Lydecke. Morì nel 1550, nel Norrbärke, Kopparberg, Svezia, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Per Hermansson Flemingが約1480年にフィンランドアスケアインで生まれた時，父Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäsは41歳，母Elin Filipsdotterは31歳であった。彼はElin Hansdotter Lydeckeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼は1550年にスウェーデンコッパルバーグノルブaルクで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Per Hermansson Fleming(이)가 대략 1480년 핀란드 아스카인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Elin Filipsdotter(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Elin Hansdotter Lydecke(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1550년 스웨덴 코파르버그 노르브a르크에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Per Hermansson Fleming nasceu aproximadamente 1480, em Askainen, Finlândia, seu pai, Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Elin Filipsdotter, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas com Elin Hansdotter Lydecke. Ele faleceu em 1550, em Norrbärke, Kopparberg, Suécia, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Per Hermansson Fleming родился около 1480 г. в местности аскеинен, Финляндия, его отцу по имени Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Elin Filipsdotter, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей от Elin Hansdotter Lydecke. Он умер в 1550 г. в местности норрбaрк, коппарбирг, Швеция в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Per Hermansson Fleming nació aproximadamente 1480, en Askainen, Finlandia, su papá, Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Elin Filipsdotter, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas con Elin Hansdotter Lydecke. Murió en 1550, en Norrbärke, Kopparberg, Suecia, a la edad de 71 años.
